message:
refactor(ffs-extent): validate extent lengths via encode helpers, drop redundant mark_count

Replaces scattered `(x as u16)` truncating casts in allocate_extent,
allocate_unwritten_extent, and split_for_mark_written with two
validating helpers:

  encode_written_len(label, len) -> Result<u16>
      nonzero, <= EXT_INIT_MAX_LEN, u16-typed

  encode_unwritten_len(label, len) -> Result<u16>
      nonzero, <= EXT_INIT_MAX_LEN - 1 (one slot reserved for the
      UNWRITTEN_FLAG bit), u16-typed, returns the value already
      OR'd with UNWRITTEN_FLAG

All callers now bubble an `FfsError::InvalidGeometry` with a
descriptive `label` when an extent length would overflow u16 or
violate the written/unwritten boundary instead of silently
truncating. Removes the last `#[expect(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]`
attributes from this module.

Drop the redundant `mark_count` argument from
`split_for_mark_written`. It was always derivable from
`mark_end - mark_start` and in some branches was carrying a stale
value (e.g. the "left-unwritten / middle-written / right-unwritten"
span-entire-range branch now computes the middle length from
mark_end - mark_start_u64 directly). All call sites (production and
proptest/unit tests) updated. The signature is now
`split_for_mark_written(&ext, mark_start, mark_end, ext_end)`.

Physical-offset arithmetic in the split paths also reverted to
computing from the original block deltas (mark_start_u64 - ext_start,
mark_end - ext_start) rather than the freshly-encoded-plus-flagged
u16 values, which had to be masked back; the new code uses u64
deltas directly and is clearer about what physical_start is tracking.

Added a guard at the top of split_for_mark_written that rejects
degenerate ranges (`mark_end <= mark_start` or `ext_end <= ext_start`)
with InvalidGeometry rather than silently producing empty extents.

Tests
-----
- New unit test `encode_unwritten_len_rejects_written_boundary`
  asserts that passing `EXT_INIT_MAX_LEN` (i.e. the written-only
  upper bound, which would collide with the UNWRITTEN_FLAG bit) to
  the unwritten encoder returns an InvalidGeometry error whose
  message mentions the 32767 limit.
- All existing split_for_mark_written call sites in unit tests and
  the five `proptest!` blocks updated to drop the `mark_count`
  argument. The proptest `prop_assume!` guards replaced `mark_count > 0`
  with the equivalent `mark_end > mark_start`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
